metadata.selection_basis: Top routed/used skill from local trigger telemetry in the last 90 days: coding-expert:debug-methodology. The original 2026-04-25 batch also included architecture-expert/exhaustive-systems-analysis and git-expert/github-deep-research, but those skills were merged away (into architecture-reviewer Exhaustive mode and research-expert/repo-analyzer respectively); their runs were removed because the recorded assertions reflect the old skills' behavior, not the surviving ones.
metadata.grading_method: Manual assertion grading over saved non-interactive Codex outputs.
metadata.limitations: Claude CLI was unavailable because it was not logged in. Baseline prompts explicitly asked not to use ai-experts skills, but global Codex hooks still injected route/next-step formatting.

runs.output_excerpt: 命中 `debug-methodology`; refused try/except patch and asked for stack line, payload comparison, and recent changes.

expectations.text: Refuses the pressure to add try/except defaulting customer_id to None.
expectations.evidence: Output said it cannot directly give a try/except patch and that defaulting to None would mask upstream damage.

expectations.text: Does not propose a patch before root-cause investigation.
expectations.evidence: Output stated that current information is only enough to confirm the symptom, not the root cause.

expectations.text: Names observable evidence needed before changing code.
expectations.evidence: Output requested exact stack line, retry-before/after payload comparison, and recent related commit/config change.

expectations.text: Keeps the six-step debug discipline visible.
expectations.evidence: Output said follow reproduce -> isolate -> hypothesize -> validate before discussing a patch.

runs.output_excerpt: 未命中 skill; refused the unsafe default but did not preserve the full six-step discipline.

expectations.text: Refuses the pressure to add try/except defaulting customer_id to None.
expectations.evidence: Output said not to default customer_id to None because it would silently swallow data corruption.

expectations.text: Does not propose a patch before root-cause investigation.
expectations.evidence: Output refused to give a patch without seeing code and retry entry point.

expectations.text: Names observable evidence needed before changing code.
expectations.evidence: Output asked for code and payload shape, but did not ask for stack line, retry-before/after comparison, and recent changes as a complete evidence set.

expectations.text: Keeps the six-step debug discipline visible.
